title: Introduction to Computing
description: An introductory course covering the fundamentals of computing, hardware, software, and basic computer operations.

title: Computer Programming 1
description: Introduction to programming concepts using a structured programming language. Topics include variables, control structures, and basic algorithms.

title: Mathematics for IT
description: Covers mathematical concepts essential for IT students including logic, sets, functions, and discrete mathematics.

title: Computer Programming 2
description: Continuation of programming concepts with focus on object-oriented programming, classes, inheritance, and polymorphism.

title: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Study of fundamental data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs along with algorithm analysis.

title: Database Management Systems
description: Introduction to database concepts, relational models, SQL, and database design including normalization.

title: Web Development
description: Covers the design and development of web applications using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks.

title: Networking Fundamentals
description: Introduction to computer networking, OSI model, TCP/IP protocols, and network administration basics.

title: Capstone Project 1
description: First phase of the capstone project where students plan, design, and prototype a real-world IT solution.

title: Capstone Project 2
description: Completion and defense of the capstone project developed in Capstone Project 1.

title: Introduction to Computer Science
description: An overview of computer science as a discipline, covering history, foundational concepts, and career paths.

title: Programming Fundamentals
description: Core programming concepts using Python. Topics include syntax, control flow, functions, and basic I/O operations.

title: Object-Oriented Programming
description: In-depth study of OOP principles using Java. Covers classes, objects, inheritance, encapsulation, and polymorphism.

title: Discrete Mathematics
description: Study of mathematical structures used in computer science including logic, sets, relations, graphs, and combinatorics.

title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduction to AI concepts including search algorithms, knowledge representation, machine learning, and neural networks.

title: Software Engineering
description: Principles and practices of software engineering including SDLC, Agile methodologies, testing, and project management.

title: Principles of Management
description: Introduction to management theories and practices including planning, organizing, leading, and controlling.

title: Business Mathematics
description: Application of mathematical concepts to business problems including interest, annuities, and financial ratios.

title: Financial Accounting
description: Fundamentals of accounting including the accounting cycle, financial statements, and basic bookkeeping.

title: Marketing Management
description: Study of marketing principles, consumer behavior, market research, and marketing mix strategies.

title: Understanding the Self
description: A general education course that explores the nature of identity, self-awareness, and personal development.

title: Purposive Communication
description: Develops communication skills for academic, professional, and social purposes in local and global contexts.

title: Child and Adolescent Development
description: Study of physical, cognitive, and socioemotional development of children and adolescents relevant to teaching.

title: The Teaching Profession
description: An overview of the teaching profession including roles, responsibilities, ethics, and legal obligations of teachers.

title: Introduction to Hospitality Management
description: Overview of the hospitality industry including hotel operations, food service, tourism, and career opportunities.
